title: Ratchet Universality and optimal suppression of shot noise in biharmonically-driven tunnel junctions
abstract: This Letter discusses two retrodictions of the law of ratchet universality which explain previous numerical and experimental results concerning the diode effect in conventional superconducting tunnel-junctions in one case, and controlled suppression of electron-hole pair generation in a tunnel junction in the other, both in the presence of biharmonic driving fields. Our study demonstrates that the ratchet universal driving field maximizes the diode's efficiency while yielding a maximal rectification range for the supercurrent, on the one hand, and optimally reduces the excess quantum noise with respect to the dc noise level, thus allowing for the efficient production of nonclassical photonic states. These results suggest that the ratchet universality law seems essential for any \textit{optimal} application of the ratchet effect, particularly in the contexts of superconducting integrated power electronics, electron quantum optics, and quantum computing.
summary: This paper introduces the law of ratchet universality to explain why specific biharmonic driving waveforms optimize the superconducting diode effect and suppress shot noise. It demonstrates that a universal waveform maximizes transport efficiency while minimizing electron-hole pair generation. These findings have significant implications for the development of superconducting power electronics and quantum computing components.
main_problem: The paper seeks to provide a theoretical justification for the 'magical' driving parameters that maximize the superconducting diode effect and minimize shot noise in biharmonically-driven tunnel junctions.
main_result: The authors establish the 'law of ratchet universality' (RU), which identifies a universal waveform that maximizes directed transport efficiency and minimizes excess quantum noise/electron-hole pair generation.
method: The study uses the theoretical framework of the law of ratchet universality, applying perturbation theory, Bessel functions, and numerical simulations of overdamped dynamics and symmetry breaking.
model_or_system: The system consists of superconducting tunnel junctions (TJs) and quantum conductors driven by biharmonic AC fields (fundamental and second harmonic) in the overdamped and adiabatic regimes.
key_observables: Diode efficiency, supercurrent rectification range, shot noise (current fluctuations), excess quantum noise, and probability of electron-hole pair creation.
important_parameters: Relative amplitude of harmonics (zeta), relative phase (theta/phi), driving frequencies (omega, 2omega), DC voltage/current, and temperature.
main_assumptions: The analysis assumes an overdamped limit for the junction dynamics and focuses on the fast-driving or adiabatic regimes.
figures_summary: Figure 1 compares diode efficiency to RU predictions; Figure 2 shows optimal biharmonic waveforms and symmetry relations; Figure 3 illustrates the boundaries of the superconducting channel; Figure 4 plots excess and current noise as functions of relative amplitude.
paper_structure: The paper introduces the problem of unexplained optimal parameters, proposes the law of ratchet universality as a unifying principle, provides analytical derivations for optimal waveforms and noise suppression, and validates these findings through numerical simulations and comparison with previous experimental/numerical results.
why_interesting: The discovery of a universal waveform for optimizing transport coherence and minimizing noise is highly relevant to quantum optics and open quantum systems, particularly for the efficient production of nonclassical photonic states.
analysis_basis: full PDF text, analyzed in chunks
